description: Cycle-004 S01 product-consumed derivation-kernel known-answer vectors (F-5: authored from the written SDD spec BEFORE the kernel exists; expected values NEVER generated from the implementation under test). CP-B correction: existence-bound vectors pin the EXACT IEEE left-fold bound_value (a hashed evidence field) + gate decision, add an underflow-region category, and the full-kernel derive/reject vectors pin the COMPLETE SDD ParameterRecord v0 / RejectionRecord shapes including exact bound_value. Binomial/CI/n*/existence-bound expectations independently computed by exact-rational BigInt reference and cross-checked against the pinned IEEE recurrence. S02 lab-consumed categories (scoring, origin-schedule/purge, manifest-enumeration) are intentionally ABSENT.

digest_conventions.canonicalization: src/receipt/canonicalize.js (JCS-subset: sorted object keys, stable array order, no insignificant whitespace)
digest_conventions.entry_digest: sha256(canonicalize(vector object with entry_digest omitted)) — covers id/consumer/category/op/input/expected/expectation_source/derivation_note/expected_digest

harness_conventions.p_and_alpha: decimal strings parsed per DR-6 to {num, den=10^d} (e.g. "0.95"→{95,100}, "0.05"→{5,100})
harness_conventions.existenceBound_edge: op existenceBound returns the pinned-left-fold JS Number; harness asserts existenceBound(n,p) === expected.bound_value (EXACT Number equality — JS canonical number serialization is part of the deterministic contract) AND (existenceBound(n,p) <= alphaFloat) === expected.passes. Applies to categories existence-bound-edge and existence-bound-underflow.
harness_conventions.throws: expected {throws:true, reason} ⇒ harness asserts the op throws (DR-5 invalid input); reason is a documentation slug
harness_conventions.kernel_projection: op deriveThresholdParameter: derive (RANKED_CANDIDATES) and insufficient_history reject vectors pin the COMPLETE SDD record (ParameterRecord v0 §Lane-L1:373-395 / RejectionRecord :400-409) incl. exact bound_value — harness deep-equals the record. The no_nontrivial_parameter vector pins the deterministic pre-existence-gate fields only (state, reason_code, evidence{window, effective_information}); existence-gate fields (bound_value, n_star, reconsideration.needed_n) are absent by design because that gate never runs, and the fuller record shape is not pinned by the Phase-0 SDD.
harness_conventions.effectiveInformation_input: input.qualifying is the F-3-ordered qualifying array (already filtered/sorted)

vectors.description: CI equal-tail branch n=20 p=0.5 α=0.05
vectors.derivation_note: Exact-rational Binomial(20,½) CDF: l=6 (F(5)=21700/2^20≈0.02069≤0.025<F(6)), u=15 (F(14)≈0.97931≥0.975>F(13)); coverage F(14)−F(5)≈0.9586≥0.95 → no widen. Cross-checked vs IEEE pmf-recurrence (agree).

vectors.description: CI equal-tail branch n=30 p=0.5 α=0.05
vectors.derivation_note: Exact-rational Binomial(30,½): l=10, u=21, coverage≥0.95 → no widen. Cross-checked vs IEEE recurrence (agree).

vectors.description: CI equal-tail naturally (1,n) n=7 p=0.5 α=0.05
vectors.derivation_note: Binomial(7,½): F(0)=1/128≈0.0078≤0.025 (l=1, F(1)>0.025), F(6)=127/128≈0.9922≥0.975 (u=7); coverage 0.9844≥0.95 → equal-tail yields (1,7), widened=false (NOT the coverage-widen path).

vectors.description: CI coverage-widen branch n=59 p=0.95 α=0.05 (asymmetric tail)
vectors.derivation_note: Exact-rational Binomial(59,0.95): equal-tail l≈53, u=59 (fallback, no j with F(j−1)≥0.975 since max F(58)=1−0.95^59≈0.9515); coverage F(58)−F(52)≈0.934<0.95 → WIDEN to (1,59), widened=true. Cross-checked vs IEEE recurrence (agree).

vectors.derivation_note: Unique integer k with DAY_MS·2^k ≤ span < DAY_MS·2^(k+1) (span_ms=0 ⇒ null); DR-1 pinned conformance value.

vectors.description: invalid span > 2^53−1 → throw
vectors.derivation_note: DR-1: span_ms > 2^53−1 (=9007199254740991) is not a safe integer ⇒ DR-5 invalid input (throw).

vectors.description: invalid negative span → throw
vectors.derivation_note: DR-1: negative span_ms ⇒ DR-5 invalid input (throw). Never coerced/clamped.

vectors.description: invalid non-integer span → throw
vectors.derivation_note: DR-1: non-integer span_ms fails Number.isSafeInteger ⇒ DR-5 invalid input (throw).

vectors.description: EI triple, 7 obs, span 60M ms (k<0 branch), 1 transition
vectors.derivation_note: events=7; span=90M−30M=60,000,000 (<DAY_MS ⇒ k=−1); median m_W=HF-1(½)=40; indicators over time order [10..70] vs 40 = [0,0,0,0,1,1,1] ⇒ transitions=1.

vectors.description: EI triple, 5 obs, span 4000 ms, 4 transitions
vectors.derivation_note: events=5; span=4000 (k: 4000·2^15≥DAY_MS, 4000·2^14<DAY_MS ⇒ −15); median of [10,20,5,30,15]=15; indicators [0,1,0,1,0] ⇒ transitions=4.

vectors.description: EI triple, 2 obs, span exactly 1 day (k=0)
vectors.derivation_note: events=2; span=86,400,000=DAY_MS ⇒ k=0; median=100; indicators [0,1] ⇒ transitions=1.

vectors.description: EI triple, single qualifying obs, span_ms=0 ⇒ log2 null
vectors.derivation_note: events=1; span_ms=0 ⇒ log2_span_days_floor=null; transitions=0 (no i≥1).

vectors.description: existence-bound edge p=0.90 n=29 (n* pass)
vectors.derivation_note: bound_value is the EXACT JS Number from the SDD-pinned recurrence (DR-4/§8.1): p_f = 90/100 (one IEEE division); p_f^n and (1−p_f)^n each by sequential left-fold multiplication (acc·=factor, i=1..n, order pinned); final addition p_f^n + (1−p_f)^n. Computed by an independent implementation of the pinned algorithm (NOT the kernel); IEEE-754 double arithmetic is deterministic ⇒ bit-identity cross-runtime. passes = (bound_value ≤ 0.05); the pinned-IEEE decision is cross-checked against the exact-rational BigInt decision ((num^n+(den−num)^n)·20 ≤ den^n) — they agree. Harness asserts existenceBound(n,p) === bound_value AND (existenceBound(n,p) ≤ 0.05) === passes.

vectors.description: existence-bound edge p=0.90 n=28 (n*−1 fail)
vectors.derivation_note: bound_value is the EXACT JS Number from the SDD-pinned recurrence (DR-4/§8.1): p_f = 90/100 (one IEEE division); p_f^n and (1−p_f)^n each by sequential left-fold multiplication (acc·=factor, i=1..n, order pinned); final addition p_f^n + (1−p_f)^n. Computed by an independent implementation of the pinned algorithm (NOT the kernel); IEEE-754 double arithmetic is deterministic ⇒ bit-identity cross-runtime. passes = (bound_value ≤ 0.05); the pinned-IEEE decision is cross-checked against the exact-rational BigInt decision ((num^n+(den−num)^n)·20 ≤ den^n) — they agree. Harness asserts existenceBound(n,p) === bound_value AND (existenceBound(n,p) ≤ 0.05) === passes.

vectors.description: existence-bound underflow p=0.90 n=350 ((1−p_f)^n underflows to 0.0)
vectors.derivation_note: Deep sequential-power / underflow case (DR-4 "underflow-region powers", §8.2). Under the pinned left-fold, (1−p_f)^n = (10/100)^350 underflows to EXACTLY 0.0 (the minor term first reaches 0.0 at n=324; IEEE-754 gradual underflow is deterministic), so bound_value = p_f^n + 0.0 = 9.65780214059184e-17 (the surviving term, exact JS Number from the pinned recurrence, independent implementation — NOT the kernel). passes = (bound_value ≤ 0.05); cross-checked vs exact-rational BigInt (agree). This pins the exact underflow outcome so a cross-runtime change in bound_value cannot pass on the Boolean gate alone.

vectors.description: existence-bound underflow p=0.95 n=249 ((1−p_f)^n underflows to 0.0)
vectors.derivation_note: Deep sequential-power / underflow case (DR-4 "underflow-region powers", §8.2). Under the pinned left-fold, (1−p_f)^n = (5/100)^249 underflows to EXACTLY 0.0 (the minor term first reaches 0.0 at n=249; IEEE-754 gradual underflow is deterministic), so bound_value = p_f^n + 0.0 = 0.0000028390805662094717 (the surviving term, exact JS Number from the pinned recurrence, independent implementation — NOT the kernel). passes = (bound_value ≤ 0.05); cross-checked vs exact-rational BigInt (agree). This pins the exact underflow outcome so a cross-runtime change in bound_value cannot pass on the Boolean gate alone.

Under the pinned left-fold, (1−p_f)^n = (1/100)^400 underflows to EXACTLY 0.0 (the minor term first reaches 0.0 at n=162; IEEE-754 gradual underflow is deterministic), so bound_value = p_f^n + 0.0 = 0.017950553275045134 (the surviving term, exact JS Number from the pinned recurrence, independent implementation — NOT the kernel). passes = (bound_value ≤ 0.05); cross-checked vs exact-rational BigInt (agree). This pins the exact underflow outcome so a cross-runtime change in bound_value cannot pass on the Boolean gate alone.

vectors.description: existence minimum n* for p=0.90, α=0.05
vectors.derivation_note: Smallest n with bound(n,p) ≤ α, found by exact-rational BigInt increment; cross-checked against IEEE left-fold increment (agree). n*(0.90)=29.

vectors.description: existence minimum n* for p=0.95, α=0.05
vectors.derivation_note: Smallest n with bound(n,p) ≤ α, found by exact-rational BigInt increment; cross-checked against IEEE left-fold increment (agree). n*(0.95)=59.

vectors.description: existence minimum n* for p=0.99, α=0.05
vectors.derivation_note: Smallest n with bound(n,p) ≤ α, found by exact-rational BigInt increment; cross-checked against IEEE left-fold increment (agree). n*(0.99)=299.

vectors.description: exact-boundary rank n=20 p=0.95 (n·p=19 exactly)
vectors.derivation_note: a=20·95+99=1999; k=(1999−99)/100=19. n·p is exactly integral; exact-integer rank pins k=19 regardless of float rounding (§8.2 named case).

vectors.description: exact-boundary rank n=100 p=0.90 (n·p=90 exactly)
float-ceil trap class n=200 p=0.95 (n·p=190 exactly)
vectors.derivation_note: a=200·95+99=19099; k=(19099−99)/100=190. Exactly-integral n·p is the class where Math.ceil(n·p_float) can shift k by 1 depending on platform ULP (DR-4 rationale, risk-2); the exact-integer form pins k=190 invariantly.

vectors.description: float-ceil trap class n=60 p=0.95 (n·p=57 exactly)
vectors.derivation_note: a=60·95+99=5799; k=(5799−99)/100=57. Exact-integer rank; float-ceil-unsafe class.

vectors.description: float-ceil trap class n=100 p=0.99 (n·p=99 exactly)
vectors.derivation_note: a=100·99+99=9999; k=(9999−99)/100=99. Exact-integer rank; float-ceil-unsafe class.

vectors.description: boundary quantile p=0.95 over [1..20] (k=19)
interior quantile p=0.5 over [5,10,15,20,30]
vectors.derivation_note: k=ceil(5·0.5)=3 → v[2]=15 (0-based order statistic).

vectors.description: interior quantile p=0.95 over [1..10]
interior rank n=10 p=0.95 (n·p=9.5, non-integral)
interior rank n=7 p=0.5 (median, n·p=3.5)
interior rank n=3 p=0.5 (n·p=1.5)
full-kernel derive → RANKED_CANDIDATES: COMPLETE ParameterRecord v0 (p=0.5, 7 obs)

uncertainty.coverage_model: distribution-free; exact if no ties, else conservative

vectors.derivation_note: qualify→window (W=min_days·DAY_MS=86.4M captures all 7 within [3,600,000, 90,000,000], n_obs=7)→EI over window {events:7, span_ms:60,000,000, log2_span_days_floor:−1, transitions:1}→missingness/degeneracy MAY-gates pass (7≥½·7; spread>0)→existence gate bound(7,½)=0.5^7+0.5^7=0.015625≤0.05 pass (n_star=6). COMPLETE ParameterRecord v0 per SDD §Lane-L1:373-395 pinned: name/units/origin/derivation(algorithm_id/version/quantile_definition/p/input_window/effective_information/quantization)/uncertainty(kind/lo=v[0]=10/hi=v[6]=70/method/coverage_model/alpha/ranks=orderStat(7,½)={l:1,u:7,widened:false})/evidence.ei_gate(gate_id/passed/bound_value=0.015625 exact/alpha/n_star=6). value=HF-1(½)=40. Every deterministic hashed field pinned incl. exact bound_value.

vectors.description: full-kernel reject → NO_INSTRUMENT no_nontrivial_parameter (pre-existence-gate MAY)
vectors.derivation_note: 6 identical values (v_min=v_max=5): qualify→window (n=6, [3,600,000, 90,000,000])→EI{events:6,span_ms:50,000,000,log2_span_days_floor:−1,transitions:0}→no_nontrivial_parameter MAY-gate (zero value spread) fires BEFORE the existence gate (SDD:358 ordering) ⇒ NO_INSTRUMENT: no_nontrivial_parameter. The existence gate never runs, so bound_value / n_star / reconsideration.needed_n are ABSENT BY DESIGN (not invented — operator instruction). Deterministic pre-gate fields pinned: state, reason_code, evidence{window, effective_information}. The fuller no_nontrivial_parameter record shape is not pinned by the Phase-0 SDD; finalized in Phase C.

vectors.description: full-kernel reject → NO_INSTRUMENT insufficient_history: COMPLETE RejectionRecord (existence gate)

reconsideration.condition: window may satisfy the existence bound after (needed_n − n) further qualifying observations

vectors.derivation_note: n_min=3 (below n*=6): window forms with n=4 (all 4 within [3,600,000, 90,000,000]); existence gate bound(4,½)=0.5^4+0.5^4=0.125>0.05 fails ⇒ NO_INSTRUMENT: insufficient_history. COMPLETE RejectionRecord per SDD:400-409 pinned: reason_code, evidence(gate_id="quantile-ci-existence", n=4, p="0.5", alpha="0.05", bound_value=0.125 EXACT, window{start_ms:3,600,000,end_ms:90,000,000}, effective_information{events:4,span_ms:55,000,000,log2_span_days_floor:−1,transitions:1}), reconsideration{needed_n=n*(½)=6, condition}. Exact bound_value pinned.

vectors.description: OFF: options with no experimental key ⇒ null
vectors.derivation_note: DR-3: absent experimental ⇒ OFF ⇒ parseExperimentalOptions returns null (no throw).

vectors.description: OFF: derivation:false ⇒ null
vectors.derivation_note: DR-3: canonical explicit-OFF form ⇒ null.

vectors.description: p malformed decimal string ⇒ throw
p out of (0,1) / bad format ⇒ throw
vectors.derivation_note: DR-3/DR-6: p must match ^0\.[0-9]{1,6}$ and satisfy 0<p<1; "1.5" fails both ⇒ throw.

vectors.description: window.min_days < 1 ⇒ throw
vectors.derivation_note: DR-3: min_days is integer ≥ 1; 0 ⇒ throw.

vectors.description: window.n_min < 2 ⇒ throw
vectors.derivation_note: DR-3: n_min is integer ≥ 2; 1 ⇒ throw.

vectors.description: unknown key inside derivation ⇒ throw
vectors.derivation_note: DR-3: only p and window recognized inside derivation; unknown key throws.

vectors.description: unknown key inside experimental ⇒ throw
vectors.derivation_note: DR-3 fail-closed: only "derivation" recognized inside experimental; the typo "derviation" must throw, never silently no-op.

vectors.description: duplicate values at duplicate timestamps (all retained)
vectors.derivation_note: Duplicates never coalesced; sort asc by ts, equal ts tiebroken by input index ⇒ [1000/5(idx2),1000/5(idx3),4000/5(idx0),4000/5(idx1)].

vectors.description: all equal timestamps, differing values, order fixed by input index
vectors.derivation_note: All ts equal ⇒ order preserved by input index (stable total order): [50,10,30]. Deterministic transitions/window downstream.

vectors.description: both fallback ts_source variants filtered (only parsed retained)
vectors.derivation_note: Qualifying requires metadata.ts_source==="parsed"; fallback_base (t=2000) and fallback_wallclock (t=4000) excluded ⇒ [1000/10,3000/30]. (Non-finite numeric values cannot be represented in a JSON vector — that branch is exercised by an in-memory Phase-C unit test, not a KAT.)

vectors.description: out-of-order + equal-ts tiebreak + non-parsed filtered
vectors.derivation_note: ts_source!=="parsed" (t=500) filtered; sort asc by ts; equal ts=1000 tiebroken by input index (v=10 idx1 before v=15 idx4) ⇒ [1000/10,1000/15,2000/20,3000/30].

vectors.description: exactly one qualifying observation (two non-parsed filtered)
vectors.derivation_note: t=7000 (fallback_wallclock) and t=2000 (fallback_base) filtered as non-parsed; only t=9000 qualifies ⇒ single-entry result [9000/9] (the span_ms=0 / one-qualifying-timestamp case for downstream EI).

vectors.description: n_min met inside min-days window (W=min_days·DAY_MS)
vectors.derivation_note: min window [90M−86.4M, 90M]=[3.6M,90M] contains all 3 (≥ n_min=3) ⇒ W=min_days·DAY_MS, start_ms=3,600,000, n=3.

vectors.description: n_min forces expansion past min-days to include the n_min-th obs
vectors.derivation_note: min window holds 3 (<n_min=4); expand W=max(min_days·DAY_MS, 90M−1M)=89M so the 4th obs (t=1M) is included; start_ms=1,000,000, n=4. Boundary inclusive [start,end].

vectors.description: fewer than n_min qualifying obs ⇒ null (gate will reject)
vectors.derivation_note: Only 3 qualifying obs at/before endMs < n_min=4 ⇒ trailingWindow returns null; the kernel then rejects (insufficient_history).

vectors.description: Pinball loss, y ≥ q̂, p=0.5
vectors.derivation_note: ρ = p_f·(y−q̂) with p_f=0.5, y≥q̂: 0.5·(10−8)=1. Exact (SDD Lane L3).

vectors.description: Pinball loss, y < q̂, p=0.5
Pinball loss asymmetry, y ≥ q̂, p=0.75
vectors.derivation_note: p_f=0.75 (exact 3/4), y≥q̂: 0.75·(10−8)=1.5. Pins the asymmetric weight.

vectors.description: Pinball loss asymmetry, y < q̂, p=0.75
vectors.derivation_note: (1−p_f)=0.25 (exact 1/4), y<q̂: 0.25·(8−5)=0.75. Pins the asymmetric weight.

vectors.description: Per-origin pinball = left-fold sum ÷ count, p=0.5
HF-1 median (exact order statistic, p=1/2), odd count
vectors.derivation_note: sorted [1,2,3]; HF-1 rank ⌈3·(1/2)⌉=2 ⇒ v[1]=2. Order statistic, no averaging (DR-4).

vectors.description: HF-1 median (order statistic), even count — lower median, no averaging
vectors.derivation_note: sorted [1,2,3,4]; HF-1 rank ⌈4·(1/2)⌉=2 ⇒ v[1]=2. Pure order statistic (never (v[1]+v[2])/2).

vectors.description: Two-sided sign test at p=1/2, 6 vs 2, no ties
vectors.derivation_note: n_eff=8, k=min(6,2)=2; exact-rational Binomial(8,½): F(2)=(1+8+28)/256=37/256; p=2·37/256=74/256=0.2890625. Cross-checked BigInt.

vectors.description: Sign-test ties dropped SEPARATELY; two-sided p clamps at 1
vectors.derivation_note: 4 ties excluded from the sign-test population (n_eff=6, not 10); k=3; 2·F(3)=84/64>1 ⇒ clamp to 1. Ties are dropped separately from rejections (F-2).

vectors.description: 95% binomial band by CDF inversion, n=20, prob=0.5
Exceedance calibration: empirical rate vs target 1−p_f, in-band, p=0.5
vectors.derivation_note: y>12 for 13..20 ⇒ 8 exceedances; empirical 8/20=0.4; target 1−0.5=0.5; band {6,14} (see lab-binomial-band-p05); 8∈[6,14] ⇒ in_band.

vectors.description: Brier diagnostic: forecast prob = 1−p_f; mean squared error, p=0.5
vectors.derivation_note: forecast=1−0.5=0.5; each term (0.5−o)²=0.25 for o∈{0,1}; mean=0.25. Diagnostic only — never primary.

vectors.description: F-2 mixed candidate/rejected run: rejection_metrics + decision_statistics; ties dropped from sign-test only
vectors.derivation_note: F-2: 2 rejections carry no Δ (excluded from median+sign-test, kept in eligible denominator). Δ over candidate-emitting: 3−2=1, 3−4=−1, 2−2=0 ⇒ [1,−1,0]; median (order-statistic ⌈3/2⌉=2 of sorted [−1,0,1]) = 0; sign-test drops the Δ=0 tie separately ⇒ n_eff=2, k=1, 2·F(1) Binom(2,½)=6/4>1⇒1. coverage 3/5=0.6.

vectors.description: Origin schedule: UTC month boundaries, purge fields, Feb-adjacency skip, locked-tail exclusion
vectors.derivation_note: Date.UTC 2021 month starts. tail_start=tMax−⌊(tMax−tMin)/5⌋=1630368000000−4181760000=1626186240000. Eligible: m−P≥tMin+1·DAY (m≥Feb1) ∧ m+H≤tail_start (m≤Jun1) ⇒ Feb..Jun. Keep-rule (gap≥H=30d): Feb kept; Mar−Feb=28d<30 ⇒ SKIP (Feb-adjacency); Apr(59d),May(30d),Jun(31d) kept. Jul/Aug excluded by the locked tail.

vectors.description: Origin made ineligible by the pre-cutoff history floor (min_days=60)
vectors.derivation_note: History floor tMin+60·DAY makes rule (a) m−P≥tMin+60·DAY ⇒ m≥tMin+90d≈Apr1; Feb1/Mar1 become history-ineligible (distinct from the tail control). Eligible/kept = Apr,May,Jun.

vectors.description: F-7 deterministic asset enumeration: POSIX-normalize + lexicographic sort (cross-platform ordering)
vectors.derivation_note: Backslashes → forward; lexicographic (UTF-16/byte) sort is the sole ordering authority (never readdir). "R"(82)<"lab"(108)<"src"(115); within lab/harness, "baselines"<"run"; within src, "classifier"<"derive". Windows and Ubuntu yield identical order.
